claim: MC-style hypothesis packets become more scientifically useful when every proposed inference is paired with explicit disconfirmation routes, measurable variables, source status, missingness handling, and boundary labels before promotion into memory, public research, or action planning.
source_status: assistant-generated semi-executable research-organization artifact; no external factual claim is asserted by this checklist
claim_status: hypothesis about evidence-organization workflow quality, not proof of scientific discovery
privacy_status: public-safe synthetic structure only; no personal, clinical, veterinary, or private identifiers included
missingness: requires unknown, not_collected, redacted, and not_applicable to remain distinct from observed absence
revision_reason: adds a falsification-focused scientific reasoning artifact to complement prior symbolic, privacy, nervous-system, and animal-care forge tests
implementation_status: semi-executable checklist usable by validators, reviewers, or future scoring scripts
testability: A future validator can score each hypothesis packet against checklist_items and reject packets with failed hard_block items or insufficient measurable_variables.
falsification_route: The claim weakens if packets using this checklist do not improve reviewer agreement, error detection, boundary compliance, or downstream test design compared with unstructured hypothesis notes.
next_executable_action: Implement validate_scientific_reasoning_checklist.py to score sample hypothesis packets against this checklist.

claim_strength_scale.1: structured hypothesis with variables but no supporting observations
claim_strength_scale.2: pattern observed in synthetic or personal notes; not externally validated
claim_strength_scale.3: externally source-grounded but not experimentally tested here
claim_strength_scale.4: testable prediction with a defined disconfirmation route
claim_strength_scale.5: replicated evidence from external methods; still reviewable and bounded

checklist_items.requirement: Packet route must remain inside research organization, question prep, experiment design, evidence map, or contradiction ledger.
Every claim must label whether its source is synthetic, user-observed, external literature, measurement-derived, or unknown.
fail_if: source_status implies external proof without citation or measurement reference

checklist_items.requirement: Unknown and not-collected data must not be treated as absence, negative evidence, or lack of risk.
fail_if: not_applicable used to hide missing required data

checklist_items.requirement: Packet must identify at least two measurable variables before being promoted from symbolic or interpretive language into a research hypothesis.
fail_if: variables cannot be observed, counted, compared, or operationally defined

checklist_items.requirement: Packet must state what observation, measurement, reviewer disagreement, or failed prediction would weaken the claim.
fail_if: claim cannot be weakened by any observation
contradictory evidence is reframed as support

checklist_items.requirement: Claim strength must match evidence strength and must not promote speculation into proof.
fail_if: claim_status says proven while evidence is synthetic
symbolic resonance is treated as factual discovery
single observation is treated as causality
private longitudinal pattern is treated as population-level finding

checklist_items.requirement: Medical and animal-care outputs must remain research organization or question-prep infrastructure.
fail_if: emergency urgency assigned by the packet

checklist_items.requirement: Every promoted packet must explain why it was revised or promoted.
fail_if: revision reason does not identify what changed
promotion occurs without reviewer or validator trace

sample_packet_stub.missingness: observed variable list plus explicitly unknown confounders
sample_packet_stub.revision_reason: converted broad insight into measurable hypothesis form
sample_packet_stub.testability: compare structured packet review scores against unstructured notes
sample_packet_stub.falsification_route: if reviewers cannot identify clearer variables or disconfirmation routes than baseline notes, checklist usefulness weakens
sample_packet_stub.next_executable_action: add this packet to a fixture and run checklist scoring
